calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-11-2019 Vacated

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Oct-24-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Roberto Domingo Quiroga, Esq. 310-277-7529 of the Law Offices of Arash Khorsandi PC for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Christopher Nolin, Esq. 415-421-3100 of Gordon Creed Kelley Holl & Sugerman for Defendant John Smith.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF ENEIDA RECINOS' MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AS AGAINST AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES 1,4,7,8,9,10 & 12 IN DEFENDANT'S ANSWER.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiff Eneida Recinos' motion for judgment on the pleadings on the first, fourth, seventh through tenth and twelfth affirmative defenses in the answer filed by defendant RJR Transportation, Inc. is denied as to all seven affirmative defenses. RJR's pleading of its affirmative defenses comports with the longstanding practice for pleading affirmative defenses in Bay Area state courts based on the well-understood premise that at the outset of a case a defendant may know the reasonable range of possible affirmative defenses but may not yet have had the opportunity to determine whether there is factual support for them. If, after a reasonable opportunity for discovery and factual investigation has expired without RJR having any factual support for some or all of this affirmative defenses, counsel for Ms. Recinos should request that counsel for RJR withdraw those affirmative defenses and, if counsel for Ms. Recinos is unsatisfied by the response, then Ms. Recinos can bring a summary adjudication motion seeking to eliminate the affirmative defenses lacking any factual support. Insufficient time has elapsed for RJR to have a reasonable opportunity for discovery and factual investigation regarding its affirmative defenses.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, Maria.Torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-24-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Roberto Domingo Quiroga, Esq. 310-277-7529 of the Law Offices of Arash Khorsandi PC for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Christopher Nolin, Esq. 415-421-3100 of Gordon Creed Kelley Holl & Sugerman for Defendant John Smith.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF ENEIDA RECINOS' DEMURRER TO THE ANSWER FILED BY DEFENDANT JOHN SMITH.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiff Eneida Recinos' demurrer to the first through twelfth affirmative defenses in the answer filed by defendant John Smith is overruled as to all twelve affirmative defenses. Mr. Smith's pleading of his affirmative defenses comports with the longstanding practice for pleading affirmative defenses in Bay Area state courts based on the well-understood premise that at the outset of a case a defendant may know the reasonable range of possible affirmative defenses but may not yet have had the opportunity to determine whether there is factual support for them. If, after a reasonable opportunity for discovery and factual investigation has expired without Mr. Smith having any factual support for some or all of this affirmative defenses, counsel for Ms. Recinos should request that counsel for Mr. Smith withdraw those affirmative defenses and, if counsel for Ms. Recinos is unsatisfied by the response, then Ms. Recinos can bring a summary adjudication motion seeking to eliminate the affirmative defenses lacking any factual support.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, Maria.Torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
